name: BeamMP Server
author: filipnone
description: Multiplayer for BeamNG.Drive. You can find detailed instructions on the BeamMP Wiki

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# BeamMP Server Installation Script (Fixed)
set -e

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

# --- Detect architecture ---
ARCH=$(uname -m)
case "$ARCH" in
  x86_64)  ARCH_NAME="x86_64" ;;
  aarch64) ARCH_NAME="arm64" ;;
  *) echo "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH"; exit 1 ;;
esac

# --- Install runtime dependencies ---
# liblua5.3 is REQUIRED by BeamMP-Server binary.
echo "Installing runtime dependencies (liblua5.3, curl, jq)..."
apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y liblua5.3-0 curl jq ca-certificates

# --- Remove any old binary ---
rm -f BeamMP-Server

# --- Fetch release metadata from GitHub API ---
LATEST_JSON=$(curl --silent "https://api.github.com/repos/BeamMP/BeamMP-Server/releases/latest")
RELEASES=$(curl --silent    "https://api.github.com/repos/BeamMP/BeamMP-Server/releases")

# --- Determine host distro version (best-effort) ---
UBUNTU_VER=""
if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then
  . /etc/os-release
  UBUNTU_VER="$VERSION_ID"
fi

# --- Asset selection with smart fallback chain ---
# Priority: Ubuntu matching host -> newest Ubuntu -> newest Debian
select_asset() {
  local RELEASE_JSON="$1"
  local URLS
  URLS=$(echo "$RELEASE_JSON" | jq -r '.assets[].browser_download_url' 2>/dev/null)

  local PATTERNS=(
    "BeamMP-Server.ubuntu.${UBUNTU_VER}.${ARCH_NAME}"
    "BeamMP-Server.ubuntu.24.04.${ARCH_NAME}"
    "BeamMP-Server.ubuntu.22.04.${ARCH_NAME}"
    "BeamMP-Server.debian.13.${ARCH_NAME}"
    "BeamMP-Server.debian.12.${ARCH_NAME}"
  )

  for PATTERN in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    # Skip empty patterns (when UBUNTU_VER is empty)
    [ -z "$PATTERN" ] && continue
    local HIT
    HIT=$(echo "$URLS" | grep -F "$PATTERN" | head -n1)
    if [ -n "$HIT" ]; then
      echo "$HIT"
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

# --- Resolve version ---
DOWNLOAD_URL=""
if [ -z "${VERSION}" ] || [ "${VERSION}" == "latest" ]; then
  echo "Using latest BeamMP server release"
  DOWNLOAD_URL=$(select_asset "$LATEST_JSON" || true)
else
  echo "Requested version: ${VERSION}"
  VERSION_CHECK=$(echo "$RELEASES" | jq -r --arg V "${VERSION}" '.[] | select(.tag_name==$V) | .tag_name')
  if [ "${VERSION}" == "${VERSION_CHECK}" ]; then
    DOWNLOAD_URL=$(select_asset "$(echo "$RELEASES" | jq -c --arg V "${VERSION}" '.[] | select(.tag_name==$V)')" || true)
  else
    echo "Version '${VERSION}' not found, falling back to latest"
    DOWNLOAD_URL=$(select_asset "$LATEST_JSON" || true)
  fi
fi

if [ -z "$DOWNLOAD_URL" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: No compatible BeamMP binary found for arch=${ARCH_NAME}"
  exit 1
fi

echo "Downloading: ${DOWNLOAD_URL}"
curl -sSL -o BeamMP-Server "${DOWNLOAD_URL}"
chmod +x BeamMP-Server

# --- Create default ServerConfig.toml ---
cat > ServerConfig.toml <<'EOF'
# BeamMP-Server config
# Docs: https://wiki.beammp.com/en/home/server-maintenance
# Get your AuthKey at https://keymaster.beammp.com

[General]
Name = 'BeamMP Server'
Port = 30814
AuthKey = ''
LogChat = true
Tags = 'Freeroam'
Debug = false
Private = true
MaxCars = 1
MaxPlayers = 8
Map = '/levels/gridmap_v2/info.json'
Description = 'BeamMP Default Description'
ResourceFolder = 'Resources'

[Misc]
ImScaredOfUpdates = false
SendErrorsShowMessage = true
SendErrors = true
EOF

# --- Create required directories ---
mkdir -p Resources

echo "-----------------------------------------"
echo "Installation completed"
echo "Binary: $(ls -lh BeamMP-Server | awk '{print $5}')"
echo "-----------------------------------------"

variables.name: Server Name
variables.description: The name of your server. Shown on the public server list.

variables.name: Server Description
variables.description: Description shown on the public server list.

variables.name: BeamMP Version
variables.description: BeamMP server version to install. Invalid values fall back to latest. See https://github.com/BeamMP/BeamMP-Server/releases

variables.name: Max Players
variables.description: Maximum number of players allowed on the server.

variables.name: Private Server
variables.description: Hide the server from the public list.

variables.name: Log Chat
variables.description: Enable chat logging in the console.
